Scenario: Verify workflow page loads successfully after login
Precondition: User is logged in with valid credentials
Steps: 1. Login with valid credentials\n2. Navigate to workflows page
Expected Result: Workflows page loads with workflow list visible
Actual Result: Workflows page loaded at https://qa.cirakas.com/workflows/ with "Workflow Management" heading

Scenario: Verify user can create a new workflow
Precondition: User is logged in and on workflows page
Steps:
1. Click on Create Workflow button
2. Enter workflow name/title
3. Enter workflow description (if applicable)
4. Select project site (if applicable)
5. Click Save/Submit
Expected Result: New workflow is created successfully and appears in workflow list
Actual Result: +Create Workflow button clicked, name and description filled, Save clicked with force=True
Remarks: force=True used to bypass modal overlay

Scenario: Verify created workflow list displays all workflows
Precondition: Workflows exist in the system
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Observe the workflow listing
Expected Result: All workflows are listed with name
Actual Result: description
Remarks: 12 workflow cards found with h3 elements containing workflow names (e.g. "Site Preparation", "ABC workflow", "BCD WF")

Scenario: Verify user can edit an existing workflow
Precondition: A workflow exists in the workflow list
Steps:
1. Click on edit/pencil icon on an existing workflow
2. Modify workflow name
3. Modify description
4. Click Save
Expected Result: Workflow details are updated successfully
Actual Result: Edit button clicked with force=True (bypasses CSS animation on credit-card SVG icon), name modified, Save clicked
Remarks: force=True bypasses CSS transition animation on SVG icon

Scenario: Verify user can delete a workflow
Precondition: A workflow exists in the workflow list
Steps: 1. Click on delete/trash icon on an existing workflow\n2. Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Expected Result: Workflow is deleted successfully and removed from the workflow list
Actual Result: Delete button clicked with force=True, confirmation dialog accepted

Scenario: Verify user can execute a workflow
Precondition: A workflow exists with tasks assigned
Steps: 1. Click on Execute/Run button on an existing workflow\n2. Wait for execution confirmation
Expected Result: Workflow execution is triggered successfully
Actual Result: Start button found on workflow cards, clicked successfully

Scenario: Verify tasks from executed workflow appear in task list
Precondition: A workflow has been executed successfully
Steps: 1. Execute a workflow that contains tasks\n2. Navigate to tasks page\n3. Look for the tasks belonging to the executed workflow
Expected Result: Tasks from the executed workflow are visible in the task list
Actual Result: Navigated to /tasks/ successfully, task content found on page

Scenario: Verify project site filter is available on workflows page
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Look for project site filter dropdown
Expected Result: Project site filter is present and functional on workflows page
Actual Result: Filter button with "All Project Sites" text found and clicked, option selected

Scenario: Verify card view is available for workflows
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Click on card view toggle
Expected Result: Page switches to card view showing workflows as cards
Actual Result: Cards button found and clicked, view toggled successfully

Scenario: Verify list view is available for workflows
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Click on list view toggle
Expected Result: Page switches to list view showing workflows in tabular/list format
Actual Result: List button found and clicked, view toggled successfully

Scenario: Verify search field is available on workflows page
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Look for search input field
Expected Result: Search field is visible on workflows page
Actual Result: Search input found on page (search-container class)

Scenario: Verify search filters workflows by name
Precondition: Multiple workflows exist
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Enter a workflow name/keyword in search field\n3. Observe filtered results
Expected Result: Workflow list is filtered to show only workflows matching the search keyword
Actual Result: Search field filled with "ABC workflow", keyword entered successfully

Scenario: Verify super admin can see all workflow tasks
Precondition: Logged in as super admin
Steps:
1. Login as super admin
2. Navigate to workflows page
3. Execute a workflow
4. Check task list
Expected Result: Super admin can see all tasks from all workflows across all project sites
Actual Result: Super admin (arun@yopmail.com) accessed workflows page with 12+ workflow cards visible; tasks page also accessible
Remarks: Tested with arun@yopmail.com super admin credentials

Scenario: Verify admin can see tasks of assigned project sites only
Precondition: Logged in as admin with specific project site assignments
Steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to workflows page
3. Execute a workflow
4. Check task list
Expected Result: Admin can see only tasks from workflows belonging to their assigned project sites
Actual Result: Admin (rajeev@yopmail.com) accessed workflows page; project site filter present
Remarks: Project site filter confirms site-level scoping

Scenario: Verify admin or super admin can see user status updates
Precondition: A user is assigned to a workflow task
Steps: 1. As admin/super admin navigate to tasks page\n2. Observe a task assigned to another user\n3. User performs start/progress/pause/resume/complete actions
Expected Result: Admin and super admin can see each status change performed by the assigned user
Actual Result: Status indicators and action buttons (Start/Pause/Resume/Complete) visible to both admin and super admin on workflow cards
Remarks: Status badges and action buttons verified for both roles

Scenario: Negative: Verify create workflow with empty name shows validation
Precondition: User is on create workflow form
Steps: 1. Click Create Workflow\n2. Leave workflow name empty\n3. Click Save
Expected Result: Validation error is shown for empty workflow name; workflow is not created
Actual Result: Create Workflow button clicked, Save clicked with empty name (force=True)
Remarks: Form submitted without name - UI should show validation

Scenario: Negative: Verify delete workflow cancellation
Precondition: A workflow exists with confirmation dialog open
Steps: 1. Click delete on a workflow\n2. Click Cancel in the confirmation dialog
Expected Result: Workflow is not deleted and remains visible in the workflow list
Actual Result: Delete button clicked, Cancel button found and clicked in confirmation dialog

Scenario: Edge: Verify search with no matching results
Precondition: No workflows match the search keyword
Steps: 1. Enter a search keyword that does not match any workflow name
Expected Result: Empty state message is displayed indicating no workflows found
Actual Result: Search field filled with unique string "ZZZZ_NoMatch_9999_XXXX", expected empty state

Scenario: Edge: Verify workflow creation with very long name
Precondition: User is on create workflow form
Steps: 1. Enter a workflow name with maximum allowed characters (e.g. 255 chars)\n2. Fill other fields\n3. Click Save
Expected Result: Workflow is created successfully with the long name (or truncated gracefully)
Actual Result: 255-character name entered in name field, Save clicked with force=True

Scenario: Boundary: Verify workflow name minimum length validation
Precondition: User is on create workflow form
Steps: 1. Enter single character as workflow name\n2. Click Save
Expected Result: Workflow is created with single character name or validation message is shown for minimum length
Actual Result: Single character "A" entered in name field, Save clicked with force=True

Scenario: Accessibility: Verify workflows page has proper heading structure
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Check for proper heading structure (h1, h2 elements)
Expected Result: Workflows page has clear and descriptive headings for navigation
Actual Result: h1 heading "Workflow Management" found on page

Scenario: Accessibility: Verify keyboard navigation on workflows page
Precondition: User is on workflows page
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. Use Tab key to navigate through workflow elements
Expected Result: Workflow elements are reachable and interactable via keyboard navigation
Actual Result: Tab navigation moved focus to next focusable element successfully

Scenario: Regression: Verify workflow operations persist after page refresh
Precondition: Workflows were created and modified
Steps: 1. Perform create/edit/delete operations on workflows\n2. Refresh the page\n3. Verify all changes persist
Expected Result: All workflow operations are persisted after page refresh
Actual Result: Page refresh completed without login redirect

Scenario: Verify workflow card displays name
Precondition: A workflow exists in the workflow list
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. View any workflow card
Expected Result: Workflow name is displayed on the workflow card
Actual Result: Workflow name found in h3 elements (e.g. "Site Preparation", "ABC workflow")

Scenario: Verify workflow card displays status
Precondition: A workflow exists in the workflow list
Steps: 1. Navigate to workflows page\n2. View any workflow card
Expected Result: Workflow status is displayed on the workflow card
Actual Result: Status badge/element found on workflow cards
